mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators looking to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the necessary components.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This command adds the Cluster API Helm chart repository.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components in the specified namespace, which is necessary for managing Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

kubectl create namespace capi-system

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.4

```

> Create a namespace for Cluster API.
> Install Cluster API with the specified version.
commands: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.4

steps.title: Install the Cluster API Provider
steps.description:
This step installs a specific provider for Cluster API, enabling it to manage clusters on your chosen infrastructure.

```bash

helm install cluster-api-provider-aws cluster-api/cluster-api-provider-aws --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.4

```

> Install the AWS provider for Cluster API.
commands: helm install cluster-api-provider-aws cluster-api/cluster-api-provider-aws --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.4

steps.title: Create a Cluster Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Cluster Custom Resource (CR) that defines the desired state of your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  controlPlaneRef:
    apiVersion: controlplane.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: KubeadmControlPlane
    name: my-cluster-control-plane
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: AWSCluster
    name: my-cluster-infra
EOF

```

> This command creates a Cluster CR for managing your cluster.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  controlPlaneRef:
    apiVersion: controlplane.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: KubeadmControlPlane
    name: my-cluster-control-plane
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: AWSCluster
    name: my-cluster-infra
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Cluster Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Cluster Custom Resource to ensure it is ready.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -A

```

> Verify that the Cluster CR is created and check its status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API components
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and may result in data loss if clusters are still managed by it. Ensure to backup any important data before proceeding.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace capi-system

kubectl delete namespace capi-system

```

> Uninstall the Cluster API Helm release.
> Delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API components
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure to backup your cluster configurations. This step upgrades the Cluster API components to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -A -o yaml > clusters-backup.yaml

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.4

kubectl get clusters -A

```

> Backup current cluster configurations.
> Upgrade Cluster API to the specified version.
> Verify that the Cluster CRs are still present and check their status.
commands: helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.4

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster: no provider found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs when the required provider is not installed.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the appropriate provider Helm chart is installed.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified Cluster CR does not exist.

**Fix:**
Verify the name and namespace of the Cluster CR.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to reconcile cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen due to misconfiguration in the Cluster CR.

**Fix:**
Check the configuration of the Cluster CR for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Helm release for Cluster API does not exist.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the Cluster API Helm chart.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and improved the reconciliation process.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API versions for Cluster and Machine resources.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to update your Cluster and Machine CRs to the new API versions.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get clusters -A' showing your cluster with the status 'Ready'.
codeSnippets: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.4
helm install cluster-api-provider-aws cluster-api/cluster-api-provider-aws --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.4

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  controlPlaneRef:
    apiVersion: controlplane.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: KubeadmControlPlane
    name: my-cluster-control-plane
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: AWSCluster
    name: my-cluster-infra
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
